summary: Heavily obfuscated bulk email/SMS sender toolkit with red-team framing
details: Package self-describes as 'Advanced email sender' with the keyword 'red-team' and ships ~46 heavily obfuscated JavaScript files in a hidden `.ad/` directory (obfuscator.io string-array + RC4 transformation across `.ad/x0.js` through `.ad/xz.js` plus `from-generator.js`, `phone-validator.js`, `sms-providers.js`, `smtp-health-cache.js`, `web-command.js`). Decoded CLI implements bulk SMS/email distribution with SMTP rotation, OAuth contact harvesting from operator-supplied Microsoft/Google accounts, Chrome webmail session injection, DKIM key generation, and redirect scanning — operator tooling for spam/phishing campaigns. Installer-side behavior: `npm install` runs a benign Windows shim postinstall; no credential theft, env-var scraping, home-directory secret enumeration, or remote payload fetch fires at install or require time. The `nolimit activate` subcommand POSTs `{key, hwid, hostname, platform}` to a publisher-controlled license server, but only when the user explicitly invokes the CLI subcommand — standard commercial license gating, not silent exfiltration. The harm vector is dual-use: there is no supply-chain attack against developers who install this, but the tool's documented purpose is bulk unsolicited messaging against third parties. Routing to human review so a maintainer can decide whether the abuse-tooling nature warrants a registry advisory; this is not a supply-chain installer-harm verdict.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
